MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION AND MOTION OF DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63023712) FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CITY AND THE SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ARE SEPARATE ENTITIES, AND THE COMPLAINT STATES NO FACTS INDICATING POTENTIAL LIABILITY BY THE CITY AS OPPOSED TO THE SCHOOL DISTRICT OR SCHOOL. (SEE, E.G., CAL. CONST. ART. IX, 14; SF CHARTER ART. VII, 8.100; EVID. CODE, 451(A); JACKSON V. BOARD OF ED. OF CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1967) 250 CAL.APP.2D 856, 858-59.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: TONYVAUGH @YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; PLAINTIFF SUBMITTED NO ARGUMENT OPPOSING THIS DEMURRER. THE SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VICARIOUS LIABILITY IS OVERRULED. THE DISTRICT COULD BE VICARIOUSLY LIABLE IF THE TEACHER'S ASSAULT AROSE FROM A WORK-RELATED CONFLICT OR EVENT, SUCH AS A DISPUTE WITH THE STUDENT DURING CLASS TIME, AND SUCH FACTS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. (SEE, E.G., LISA M. V. HENRY MAYO NEWHALL MEMORIAL HOSPITAL (1995) 12 CAL.4TH 291, 300; CARR V. WM. C. CROWELL CO. (1946) 28 CAL.2D 652; RODGERS V. KEMPER CONSTR. CO. (1975) 50 CAL.APP.3D 608, 621; STANSELL V. SAFEWAY STORES (1941) 44 CAL.APP.2D 822; CMPLT. 19-20.) - ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RRICHARD B. ULMER , CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: TONYVAUGHN @YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Katie Mciver,Esq., Harris, personal Injury Lawyers, Inc 408-512-3600, appearing for Plaintiff;
Marina B. Pitts, Law Offices of Leone & Alberts, 415-925-974-8800, appearing for Defendant San Francisco USD;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The City and the San Francisco Unified School District are separate entities, and the complaint states no facts indicating potential liability by the City as opposed to the school district or school. (See, e.g., Cal. Const. art. IX, 14; SF Charter art. VII, 8.100; Evid. Code, 451(a); Jackson v. Board of Ed. of City of Los Angeles (1967) 250 Cal.App.2d 856, 858-59.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Tonyvaugh @yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Katie Mciver,Esq., Harris, personal Injury Lawyers, Inc 408-512-3600, appearing for Plaintiff;
Marina B. Pitts, Law Offices of Leone & Alberts, 415-925-974-8800, appearing for Defendant San Francisco USD;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to the first cause of action for negligent supervision is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; plaintiff submitted no argument opposing this demurrer.

The school district's demurrer to the third cause of action for vicarious liability is OVERRULED. The district could be vicariously liable if the teacher's assault arose from a work-related conflict or event, such as a dispute with the student during class time, and such facts are adequately pled. (See, e.g., Lisa M. v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (1995) 12 Cal.4th 291, 300; Carr v. Wm. C. Crowell Co. (1946) 28 Cal.2d 652; Rodgers v. Kemper Constr. Co. (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 608, 621; Stansell v. Safeway Stores (1941) 44 Cal.App.2d 822; Cmplt. 19-20.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Rrichard B. Ulmer , Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Tonyvaughn @yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
